metadata.description: Compact, source-grounded catalog of Apple Core AI models, artifacts, capabilities, modalities, runtime metadata, device support and sources.
metadata.name: Core AI Catalog

models.name: Qwen3.5-0.8B
models.notes: Top-1 exact vs Hugging Face reference according to upstream.

models.name: Qwen3.5-2B
models.notes: Top-1 exact vs Hugging Face reference according to upstream.

models.name: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
models.notes: Mac-only MoE; custom gather_qmm Metal kernel.

models.name: GLM-4.7-Flash
models.notes: MoE + MLA; custom gather_qmm Metal kernel.

models.name: Gemma 4 E2B
models.notes: Text decoder; upstream reports 8/8 exact vs HF.

entrypoint.init_pattern: let engine = try await CoreAIRunner(contentsOf: bundleURL).makeInferenceEngine()
let vision = try await AIModel(contentsOf: visionURL); let decoder = try await AIModel(contentsOf: decoderURL)

decoding.detokenization: greedy decode to eos id 1; detokenize with tokenizer/ (LlamaTokenizerFast)
decoding.format_selector: fixed prompt prefix (assets/prompt_input_ids.i32) — output interleaves markdown, HTML tables and LaTeX formulas; not prompt-selectable

models.name: Unlimited-OCR
models.notes: Document OCR to markdown; tables to HTML; formulas to LaTeX.

models.name: Qwen2.5-Omni-3B Audio
models.notes: Audio understanding; describes sounds, not literal transcript.

models.name: EmbeddingGemma 300M
models.notes: Text embeddings for on-device RAG / semantic search.

entrypoint.init_pattern: let model = try await AIModel(contentsOf: aimodelURL)

decoding.format_selector: last-token pooling, L2-normalized; MRL-truncatable to 32..1024 dims

entrypoint.init_pattern: let model = try await AIModel(contentsOf: aimodelURL)

decoding.coordinate_convention: boundingBox: pixel CGRect, top-left origin (decoded from 300 normalized cxcywh queries; no NMS)
decoding.label_vocab_ref: COCO 91-id logit columns; ObjectDetectionLabels.coco default mapping

decoding.coordinate_convention: boundingBox: pixel CGRect, top-left origin (decoded from 300 normalized cxcywh queries; no NMS)
decoding.label_vocab_ref: COCO 91-id logit columns; ObjectDetectionLabels.coco default mapping

decoding.coordinate_convention: boundingBox: pixel CGRect, top-left origin (decoded from 300 normalized cxcywh queries; no NMS)
decoding.label_vocab_ref: COCO 91-id logit columns; ObjectDetectionLabels.coco default mapping

decoding.coordinate_convention: boundingBox: pixel CGRect, top-left origin (decoded from 300 normalized cxcywh queries; no NMS)
decoding.label_vocab_ref: COCO 91-id logit columns; ObjectDetectionLabels.coco default mapping

entrypoint.init_pattern: let model = try await AIModel(contentsOf: aimodelURL)
let model = try await AIModel(contentsOf: aimodelURL)
let model = try await CoreAILanguageModel(resourcesAt: bundleURL)

decoding.detokenization: embedded tokenizer (Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B, vocab 151936); LanguageModelSession.respond(to:) returns detokenized text

entrypoint.init_pattern: let pipeline = try await Flux2Pipeline(from: bundleURL, config: configSource, mode: decodeResolution)
let segmenter = try await ImageSegmenter(resourcesAt: bundleDir)

preprocessing.resize: any size; the engine resizes internally

decoding.coordinate_convention: masks decoded to input-image pixel space (SegmentationPostprocessor.decode)

entrypoint.init_pattern: let model = try await AIModel(contentsOf: aimodelURL)

preprocessing.normalization: WhisperFeatureExtractor log-mel: 128 mel bins, n_fft 400, hop_length 160

decoding.detokenization: WhisperTokenizer (tokenizer/); autoregressive decode in a fixed 128-token window

models.name: Whisper large-v3-turbo
models.notes: Bundle includes tokenizer and mel filterbank.

models.name: MiniCPM5-1B
models.notes: Hybrid Think/No-Think reasoning; 128K context; int8 lossless (24/24 exact vs HF).

models.name: FastContext-1.0-4B
models.notes: Repository-exploration agent (Microsoft); Qwen3-4B arch; AOT h18p; ANE unsupported.

models.name: BitCPM-8B
models.notes: First 1.58-bit ternary LLM; custom 2-bit packed-GEMM Metal kernel; ~2.1 GB resident on iPhone.

models.name: Holo2-4B
models.notes: GUI-grounding / computer-use VLM (H Company); Qwen3-VL-4B backbone; screenshot → click coordinates.

models.name: ColModernVBERT
models.notes: Visual document retriever; ColBERT/MaxSim late-interaction; ModernBERT + SigLIP2.

models.name: Qwen3-ASR-1.7B
models.notes: Zoo's first ASR; AuT encoder + Qwen3 decoder; 52 languages.

models.name: Parakeet-TDT-0.6B
models.notes: First transducer/TDT (RNN-T); NVIDIA FastConformer + LSTM predictor; 25 EU languages; iPhone 47.9× realtime.

models.name: VoxCPM2 2B
models.notes: 2B successor at 48 kHz; MiniCPM4 28L LM + LocDiT-12L flow-matching + 48 kHz AudioVAE.

models.name: YOLOX-S
models.notes: First single-stage anchor-free detector (YOLO family); CSPDarknet + PAFPN; per-class NMS; 208 FPS on M4 Max GPU.

models.name: TripoSplat
models.notes: Single image → 3D Gaussian splats; DINOv3 ViT-H + 20-step flow-matching DiT; .ply/.splat → RealityKit / MetalSplatter; VAST; Mac-only.

models.name: LTX-Video 2B distilled
models.notes: First video model; T5-XXL + 8-step flow-matching DiT + causal video VAE; 512×768×49f ~14s Mac GPU; Lightricks; Mac-only.

models.name: Qwen3 1.7B
models.notes: Fills the missing 1.7B rung in the dense Qwen3 Core AI line. GPU-only — ANE export omitted (loads but fails on iOS 27).

models.name: CLIP ViT-B/32
models.notes: Official recipe CLIP export (models/clip/export.py); fp16 static; text padded to 77 tokens for free-text queries. Image+text → cosine similarity.

models.name: LLaDA-8B dLLM
models.notes: First diffusion LM (dLLM) on Core AI. Masked bidirectional text generation, no KV cache. int4 viable (not a cliff). ~38 tok/s at threshold 1.0.

models.name: BitVLA
models.notes: First VLA / robotics model on Core AI. 1.58-bit ternary VLA: BitNet LLM + BitSigLIP vision → 7-DoF robot action. Generalized ternary kernel (arbitrary K/N).

models.name: Stable Audio Open Small
models.notes: First generative audio model. Latent diffusion: T5 encoder + DiT (8-step rectified-flow) + Oobleck VAE. ~11s 44.1 kHz stereo in ~0.4s on M4 Max (≈30× real-time).

models.name: Whisper large-v3-turbo (CarstenL)
models.notes: Independent conversion with split encoder/decoder .aimodel bundles. 99 languages.

models.name: Whisper tiny.en (Intiser)
models.notes: English-only Whisper tiny variant. Minimal footprint ASR.

models.name: Gemma 4 12B IT Multimodal (warshanks)
models.notes: First multimodal Gemma 4 conversion: text+image+audio input via encoder-free architecture. Custom conversion recipe extending coreai-models. INT4 block-32 weight-only decoder. Multi-function bundle (main, prefill_multimodal, embed_vision, embed_audio).

models.name: Gemma 4 12B IT Assistant (MTP draft)
models.notes: Multi-token-prediction draft model for speculative decoding with Gemma 4 12B. Cross-attends main model KV cache (layers 46/47) zero-copy. ~3ms/step on M4 Max vs ~21ms for the 12B. Companion to warshanks/gemma-4-12B-it-coreai.

models.name: VibeVoice 0.5B
models.notes: Streaming TTS with 0.5B Qwen2 backbone. 10.2x RTF on M4 Max. Multi-bundle pipeline: LM + diffusion sampler + acoustic decoder/connector. Microsoft VibeVoice original weights.

models.name: VibeVoice 1.5B
models.notes: Multi-speaker TTS with 1.5B Qwen2 backbone. INT8 LM. 4.99x RTF on M4 Max.

models.name: VibeVoice 7B
models.notes: High-quality multi-speaker TTS with 7B Qwen2 backbone. INT8/4 LM. 2.37x RTF on M4 Max.

models.name: VibeVoice ASR
models.notes: On-device transcription, Qwen2-7B. INT8/4 LM. 11.1x RTF on M4 Max. Microsoft VibeVoice ASR original weights.

models.name: Nemotron 3.5 ASR Streaming 0.6B
models.notes: NVIDIA FastConformer + RNN-T streaming ASR. Cache-aware 320ms encoder mode. AOT artifacts for iOS (A19 h18p) + macOS (M3-M5). Companion Swift package mweinbach/NemotronCoreAI. Multiple quantization variants (int8, lut6, lut8). 174 files.

models.name: Z-Image Turbo
models.notes: Tongyi-MAI Z-Image Turbo text-to-image. GPU (fast-512) and ANE (ane-1024, hoisted-adaLN) bundles at 512 and 1024 resolution. For CoreAIStudio on macOS/iOS/iPadOS/visionOS.

models.name: Real-ESRGAN x4v3
models.notes: 4x super-resolution (SRVGGNetCompact). fp16, 256 tile size. For CoreAIStudio.

models.name: EfficientSAM3-TinyViT
models.notes: Lightweight promptable segmentation. Hosted by Lenitas app distribution. 392MB total with tokenizer. SHA-256 verified.

models.name: RWKV-7 Goose 1.5B
models.notes: First pure-recurrent / linear-attention LLM on Core AI. No attention, no KV cache — O(1) per-token decode with constant memory and unbounded context. WKV7 delta-rule matrix-state time-mix + sqrelu channel-mix. int8 weight-only quant (FFN only; recurrence projections kept fp16).

entrypoint.init_pattern: let model = try await AIModel(contentsOf: aimodelURL)

models.name: V-JEPA 2 ViT-L (SSv2)
models.notes: First video world-model on Core AI. JEPA architecture (joint-embedding predictive) from Meta — learns video representations by predicting masked spatiotemporal patches. fp16, AOT h18p specialized. Trained on Something-Something v2 (action recognition). ViT-L backbone.

models.name: Ornith 1.0 9B
models.notes: First agentic-coding model in the ecosystem. Qwen3.5 hybrid decoder (32 layers, 3:1 GatedDeltaNet/attention interleave) trained by DeepReinforce to jointly solve coding tasks and construct orchestration scaffolds. int8hu ship recipe delivers 48.3 tok/s decode on M4 Max. int4lin variant reaches 58.9 tok/s (+22%), 7.5 GB. 24/24 exact match vs fp32 oracle on both quantizations. Dense MLP(12288), hidden 4096, untied 248320-vocab lm_head. Max context 8192.

metadata.name: Core AI Catalog terminology
metadata.notes: Verified Apple AI terminology reference layer. Each term cites an official Apple source and is tagged by ecosystem layer. Reference only -- not affiliation or legal claims.

terms.definition: Personal intelligence system across Apple devices, powered by Apple Foundation Models; provides personal context, app actions and on-screen awareness.
terms.label: Apple Intelligence

terms.definition: System visual-understanding capability; the 2026 update adds image input to the on-device model for on-device visual tasks.
terms.label: Visual Intelligence

terms.definition: Image-generation experience and developer API in the Apple Intelligence ecosystem.
terms.label: Image Playground

terms.definition: WWDC26 framework for bringing PyTorch models to production on Apple Silicon (LLMs and generative AI); successor to Core ML for generative AI; exports the .aimodel format.
terms.label: Core AI

terms.definition: Native Swift API giving access to Apple Foundation Models (on-device and Private Cloud Compute) and to any provider with a Swift package conforming to the LanguageModel protocol.
terms.label: Foundation Models framework

terms.definition: Framework for integrating traditional ML models into apps; predates Core AI and is superseded by it for generative AI; uses .mlpackage / .mlmodel.
terms.label: Core ML

terms.definition: Open-source array framework for ML on Apple Silicon (research, training, fine-tuning, inference) with unified memory and lazy evaluation; a parallel research/training layer, not a Core AI dependency.
terms.label: MLX

terms.definition: Framework that connects app content and actions to Apple Intelligence, Siri and Spotlight through schemas, entities and intents.
terms.label: App Intents

terms.definition: Framework for image analysis such as detection, recognition and tracking; Vision tools can be called by the model during generation.
terms.label: Vision

terms.definition: Framework for speech recognition and audio analysis on Apple platforms.
terms.label: Speech

terms.definition: WWDC26 Swift framework (sessions 298/299/335) for measuring intelligence feature quality. API: subject(from:), ModelSample, Metric, Evaluator, ModelJudgeEvaluator, ScoreDimension, TrajectoryExpectation, ToolCallEvaluator. Runs in Swift Testing, supports macOS/iOS/watchOS/visionOS, on-device or against Private Cloud Compute.
terms.label: Evaluations framework

terms.definition: GPU programming framework; backs the custom Metal kernels some Core AI model conversions require.
terms.label: Metal

terms.definition: Apple's proprietary models (on-device and server via Private Cloud Compute) that power Apple Intelligence; third generation (AFM 3) at WWDC26; not open weights and not downloadable.
terms.label: Apple Foundation Models

terms.definition: Apple's privacy-preserving server infrastructure for running larger Foundation Models off-device.
terms.label: Private Cloud Compute

terms.definition: Public Swift protocol a provider implements so any model (Apple, Claude, Gemini, local) can back a LanguageModelSession without changing app logic.
terms.label: LanguageModel protocol

terms.definition: Session object that runs prompts and tools against a LanguageModel in the Foundation Models framework.
terms.label: LanguageModelSession

terms.definition: Model invocation of app-provided tools (including Vision tools) during generation.
terms.label: Tool calling

terms.definition: Semantic index that App Intents entity schemas contribute to, so Siri can surface app content with attribution back to the app.
terms.label: Spotlight semantic index

terms.definition: On-device artifact format produced by Core AI from a PyTorch or open model; auto-specializes to the current hardware and OS version on first load (model cache).
terms.label: aimodel format

terms.definition: Core ML model package format.
terms.label: mlpackage format

terms.definition: Open tensor-serialization format commonly used for original open-model weights on Hugging Face.
terms.label: safetensors format

terms.definition: Python package for converting models from training frameworks to Core ML.
terms.label: Core ML Tools

terms.definition: Core AI tooling that converts prepared PyTorch models into the .aimodel format.
terms.label: Core AI PyTorch Extensions

terms.definition: App for visualization and numeric debugging of Core AI models, tracing tensor values back to Python source.
terms.label: Core AI Debugger

terms.definition: Apple adapter that wraps a Core AI .aimodel bundle into a LanguageModel conforming to the Foundation Models framework, enabling session.respond / stream / Tool usage with one-line integration.
terms.label: CoreAILanguageModel

terms.definition: FoundationModels.Tool that turns the Core Spotlight index into a retrieval tool for a LanguageModelSession, enabling on-device RAG. Works behind any LanguageModel, not just Apple's system model.
terms.label: SpotlightSearchTool

terms.definition: CoreAIKit abstraction for running multi-graph models (encoders, transducers) as stateless .aimodel bundles via AIModel.run, without the full LLM runtime. Used by non-LLM models like Parakeet TDT, ColModernVBERT, YOLOX.
terms.label: CoreAIKit GraphModel

terms.definition: WWDC26 mechanism to declare multiple profiles (model + instructions + tools + modifiers) within a single LanguageModelSession and switch between them as the conversation evolves. Enables on-device model routing.
terms.label: DynamicProfile

terms.definition: Swift package from the coreai-models project providing higher-level abstractions for running .aimodel bundles: KitLanguageModel (LanguageModel conformance), GraphModel (multi-graph encoders/transducers), TextEmbedder, ImageTextEncoder, and the CoreAIRunner LLM driver. The primary programmatic interface to Core AI artifacts.
terms.label: CoreAIKit

terms.definition: An alternate Core AI GPU execution engine that fuses decode operations into a pipelined graph, achieving higher throughput than the stock engine. Used by most zoo LLMs. Identified in benchmarks as 'coreai-pipelined'. The stock engine is 'coreai' without pipelining.
terms.label: coreai-pipelined engine

terms.definition: Ahead-of-time compilation of a Core AI .aimodel bundle into a .aimodelc for a specific hardware architecture (e.g. h18p for iPhone 17/18). Required when the model graph is too large to specialize on-device within reasonable time. Compiled via 'xcrun coreai-build compile --preferred-compute gpu --architecture h18p'.
terms.label: AOT compilation (.aimodelc)

terms.definition: Custom Metal kernel for grouped-query matrix multiplication used by MoE (Mixture-of-Experts) models on Core AI. Reads only the routed experts instead of the full weight matrix, enabling large MoE models (Qwen3.6-35B, GLM-4.7-Flash, LFM2.5-8B-A1B) to run on iPhone.
terms.label: gather_qmm Metal kernel

terms.definition: Host-side sampling loop for flow-matching diffusion models (FLUX.2, LTX-Video, VoxCPM). The neural networks run as Core AI bundles; only the data-dependent control flow (scheduling, timestep iteration) stays on host. The standard zoo pattern: convert heavy nets, keep control flow in Python.
terms.label: FlowMatch sampler

terms.definition: The intermediate representation produced by coreai-torch's TorchConverter. Stored as main.mlirb inside the .aimodel bundle, alongside metadata.json and main.hash. The IR is lowered by the Core AI compiler to GPU/ANE/CPU code.
terms.label: Core AI IR (.mlirb)

terms.definition: Directory structure for LLM .aimodel bundles: metadata.json (kind 'llm', assets.main, language.{tokenizer,vocab_size,max_context_length,function_map}) + tokenizer/ + the .aimodel. Required by the pipelined engine and Foundation Models integration.
terms.label: LanguageBundle

terms.definition: The on-device first-run compilation of a .aimodel for the specific hardware, managed via AIModelCache / AIModel.specialize(). Converts the portable IR to a compiled graph for the device's GPU/ANE/CPU. AOT-compiled (.aimodelc) bundles skip this step entirely.
terms.label: On-device specialization (AIModelCache)

terms.definition: Apple's term for k-means codebook compression: each weight maps to an index into a LUT of 2^n_bits centroids. Per-channel palettization consistently beats per-channel quantization by ~15-19 dB. Part of coreai-optimization (coreai-opt).
terms.label: Palettization (k-means LUT)

terms.definition: Foundation Models macro for guided/structured generation: annotated Swift structs produce a JSON schema that constrains model decoding. Works with LanguageModelSession. Requires engine logits, so GPU-pipelined bundles need the adapter path.
terms.label: @Generable

terms.definition: Beta workaround for the MPSGraph in-graph KV-write bug (FB23024751): express the KV cache as model input/output instead of a Core AI state, append with torch.cat, attend with masked SDPA, and write the new column back on the host between steps. Superseded by the input-mask escape and pipelined engine for dynamic models.
terms.label: Host-cache KV workaround

terms.definition: Custom Metal kernel that replaces the stock MPSGraph SDPA for full-attention models with ≥16 heads × head_dim 512. The stock SDPA overflows the GPU scratch heap on large Q tensors. Required by Gemma 4 12B/31B dense models to run on the pipelined engine.
terms.label: Flash-decode Metal kernel

terms.definition: Instruments template for profiling Core AI inference and specialization timing. Pairs with the Core AI Debugger (graph visualization) and the in-Xcode debug gauge (streaming Core AI activity).
terms.label: Core AI Instruments

terms.definition: Ahead-of-time compiled output of a .aimodel for a specific hardware architecture. Produced by 'xcrun coreai-build compile'. Embeds the precompiled MPSGraph so load is near-instant. Required for iOS deployment of large models. Architecture named by GPU family (e.g. h18p = iPhone 17/18 class).
terms.label: .aimodelc (AOT-compiled bundle)

conversion_sources.notes: Converts ONNX models directly to .aimodel. PyPI: coreai-onnx. Independent from Apple's PyTorch path.
conversion_sources.title: devin-lai/coreai-onnx

conversion_sources.notes: Captures MLX graphs, lowers to CoreAI MLIR, writes .aimodel. PyPI: mlx2coreai.
conversion_sources.title: lucasnewman/mlx2coreai

conversion_sources.notes: On-device agentic API demos running CoreAI zoo bundles through Foundation Models.
conversion_sources.title: john-rocky/agent-demos

framework_sources.notes: PyTorch Extensions (coreai-torch) — TorchConverter, composite_ops, Metal kernels. PyPI: coreai-torch.
framework_sources.title: apple/coreai-torch

framework_sources.notes: Core AI Optimization (coreai-opt) — quantization, palettization, pruning for Apple Silicon deployment. PyPI: coreai-opt.
framework_sources.title: apple/coreai-optimization
